backtrace.c(3) Library Functions Manual backtrace.c(3)

NAME

backtrace.c - Backtrace stubs for WASM (not available in browser environment).

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef bool(* backtrace_frame_filter_t) (const char *frame)

Functions


void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t *bt)
Capture current stack into bt. void backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
Capture and symbolize in one call. void backtrace_free (backtrace_t *bt)
void backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t *bt)
Free backtrace symbols. void backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols. void backtrace_print (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file. void backtrace_print_simple (int skip_frames)
Print backtrace of the current call stack.

Detailed Description

Backtrace stubs for WASM (not available in browser environment).

Definition in file platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* backtrace_frame_filter_t) (const char *frame)

Definition at line 42 of file platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.

Function Documentation

void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t * bt)

Capture current stack into bt. Calls platform_backtrace() to capture raw return addresses. Does not symbolize (symbols remain NULL).

Parameters

bt Backtrace structure to fill (must not be NULL)

Definition at line 9 of file platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.

9                                         {
10   if (bt) {
11     bt->count = 0;
12     bt->symbols = NULL;
13   }
14 }




References backtrace_t::count, and
    backtrace_t::symbols.






void
  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Capture and symbolize in one call. Convenience function combining
    backtrace_capture() and backtrace_symbolize().




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure to fill and symbolize (must
  not be NULL)


Definition at line 16 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.


16                                                       {
17   if (bt) {
18     bt->count = 0;
19     bt->symbols = NULL;
20   }
21 }






References backtrace_t::count, and
    backtrace_t::symbols.






void
  backtrace_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Definition at line 23 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.


23                                      {
24   if (bt) {
25     bt->count = 0;
26     bt->symbols = NULL;
27   }
28 }






References backtrace_t::count, and
    backtrace_t::symbols.






void
  backtrace_print (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bt, int
  skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file.
    Intelligently formats and routes backtraces to multiple destinations:


    
  
  • stderr: Colored output with semantic highlighting (for interactive
      debugging)
    • 
  
  • log file (if logging): Plain ASCII without ANSI codes (for file
      parsing)
    • 



This dual-output design ensures readable terminal output during
    development while keeping log files clean and searchable in production.








Output
  Format


Terminal output example:




=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
[1] 0x7f1234567800 in process_request() [src/network.c:128]
[2] 0x7f1234567700 in send_data() [src/io.c:67]



Log file (same content, no colors):




=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
...



Parameters


label Header label (e.g., "Backtrace",
  "Leak trace", "Assertion failure")


bt Backtrace to print (must not be NULL, must be symbolized via
  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize)


skip_frames Number of frames to skip from start (0 = include all, 1 =
  skip first frame, etc.)


max_frames Maximum frames to print (0 = unlimited, 5 = first 5 frames
  only)


filter Optional callback to selectively skip frames (e.g., filter
  internal frames) Return true to skip frame, false to include. NULL = no
  filtering.


Note


Backtrace must be symbolized. Unsymbolized (raw hex)
  traces are less useful.



Frame filtering is useful to focus on application code,
  excluding:


    
  
  • lib/platform internal calls
    • 
  
  • lib/log infrastructure
    • 
  
  • Exception handling wrappers
    • 





See also


backtrace_format() for buffer-based output (safe
  for log system)


Definition at line 44 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.


45                                                       {
46   (void)label;
47   (void)bt;
48   (void)skip_frames;
49   (void)max_frames;
50   (void)filter;
51   // No-op for WASM
52 }










void
  backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols() to convert raw addresses to symbol
    strings. This is a no-op if symbols are already set (already
  symbolized).




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure to symbolize (must not be
  NULL)


Definition at line 37 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.


37                                           {
38   (void)bt;
39   // No-op for WASM
40 }










void
  backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Free backtrace symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy() to free the symbols array. Nulls
    the symbols pointer after freeing.




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure (must not be NULL)


Definition at line 30 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/backtrace.c.


30                                        {
31   if (bt) {
32     bt->count = 0;
33     bt->symbols = NULL;
34   }
35 }






References backtrace_t::count, and
    backtrace_t::symbols.
